feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content:
We have a requirement related to Credit Management in SAP S/4HANA RISE.

Requirement:
If a customer’s overdue is ≥ 90 days, the system should not allow creation of a Sales Order. This restriction should be applicable only for Cement Sales. For other materials, no change is required and the process should remain as is.

Current Business Scenario:

  • We are currently using credit management at the delivery level (Credit Group 02 – Delivery).
  • There is no restriction during Sales Order creation; all checks are applied at the time of delivery creation.
  • For oldest open items, a limit of 180 days is maintained, beyond which the system restricts processing.

New Requirement:
If the customer’s oldest open item is greater than 90 days, the system should not allow Sales Order creation specifically for Cement materials. For other materials (such as TMT, Wire Rod, etc.), the process should continue as per the current business.

Kindly suggest how this requirement can be fulfilled.
Also, please advise whether creating a separate order type for Cement Sales is required, as currently we are using the same order type for all domestic sales.

@Lakshmipathi Sir, please suggest & let me know if any additional info required from my end.

SD (Sales and Distribution) SD Billing 

Regards,

Soumya Nath.

entry.title: Billing Condition Type to GL Account CDS view for creating custom sales register in sap public cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to create a custom report for Sales register. Which contains the billing document item details along with the condition type and the GL used to post the condition amount.

I am planning to develop this in the Eclipse.

I have tried to achieve the field by joining multiple CDS view as below. But unable to get the expected result.

I_BillingDocumentItem
I_BillingDocumentItemPrcgElmnt
I_SalesDocument
I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

If anyone from the community please help me to achieve this requirement.

entry.title: Completed order lignes without expected quantity and without a rejection reason
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP and would appreciate your guidance.

In a sales order, a line item is automatically set to status “Completed”, even though:

  • the delivered quantity is lower than the ordered quantity, and
  • no rejection reason is applied on the item.

I am trying to understand the root cause behind this system behavior.

My question is:
How can I identify WHY SAP decided to close the line?

Any guidance on how to analyze this step‑by‑step (or which transactions / tables / logs to check) would be very helpful.

Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: express document update was terminated received from author
entry.content:
hello experts 

i created value contract and go to display but this pop-up comes (express document update was terminated received from author ) and automatically value contract changed customer number and material changed same thing was happen when i was creating return order process literally i was working for one weak to now exact root of error i was failing again and again but i cant get the right path to solve this error.

SAP community is the last hope. 

please correct me to solve the error so i keep continuing my practice.

thanks and regards, 

vishal

entry.title: Where is displayed PRODCOM field?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'd like to know if in Sap ECC and S4  there is a report or transaction to display the  PRODCOM field.

 

Thank you

entry.title: Difference in VAT Tax – SD vs. FI
entry.content:
The issue concerns different VAT amounts on the invoice and the accounting document resulting from different calculation methods. The invoice is issued in the currency EUR, but the VAT is in the local currency PLN.

Invoice:

  1. VAT is first calculated for each line item in EUR.
  2. Rounding differences of conditions are summed and added to the item with the highest VAT amount.
  3. Then the total amount of the line items including the rounding difference, i.e. 8,722.88 EUR, is converted using the exchange rate 4.2645 PLN.
  4. The VAT in PLN amounts to 37,198.72.

     

Accounting Document:

  1. The calculated VAT in EUR, identical to the invoice, is converted for each line item into PLN using the exchange rate 4.2645 and rounded to two decimal places.
  2. Then all line items in PLN are summed, resulting in 37,201.30 PLN.
  3. The difference compared to the invoice (2.58 PLN) arises because when calculating VAT in PLN for each line item separately, there is no rounding of condition differences for this currency.

Does anyone have any idea how to solve this?

entry.title: SAP SD Rollout for New Company Code in Different Country
entry.content:
I am currently working on an SAP SD rollout for a client. We have successfully implemented SAP for their operations in Country X, and we are now expanding the solution to Country Y as a new branch with a separate Company Code.

The business processes in both countries are virtually identical (Standard Sales, Returns, Credit Memos, etc.).I know i have to create different sales org. For sure.I am seeking advice on the best architectural approach regarding the following:

1. Sales Document Types:

Since the business processes are the same, is it recommended to create new Sales Document Types (e.g., ZYOR for Country Y vs. ZXOR for Country X)? Or is it better practice to reuse the existing Document Types and simply assign them to the new Sales Areas? I am concerned about maintaining a clean configuration and whether separate Document Types are necessary for different Company Codes/Countries if the logic remains the same.

2. Distribution Channels:

Both countries operate using "Local" and "Export" sales. Should I create country-specific Distribution Channels (e.g., L1/E1 for Country X and L2/E2 for Country Y)? Or should I use common, unified Distribution Channels (e.g., 10 for Local and 20 for Export) across both Sales Organizations?

entry.title: Order Related Intercompany billing
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need some help with the order-related intercompany process.

In the standard intercompany process, we usually create a delivery. However, in our case, we want to set up an intercompany process for service items, where no delivery will be created.

I have searched extensively but could not find any end-to-end documentation for configuring this scenario.

Could someone please help me with the complete end-to-end configuration steps for setting up the intercompany process for service items without delivery?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Master Data and Price Procedure from Sales order to invoice
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

I have a question about the OTC flow (Sales Order → Delivery → Invoice) and how pricing behaves when copy control is set to not re-determine prices during invoice creation.

In this setup, the system simply copies the pricing data from the sales order — even if some master data changes between the delivery and invoice steps, including condition types that depend on that master data.

One workaround I'm thinking of: manually update the master data on the sales order and recalculate pricing (VA02), then update the relevant field on the delivery (VL02N) if it exists there — and then create a fresh invoice with the updated data.

My question is: if the delivery is already completed/goods issued, can doing this process cause any issues or impact in other steps of the flow?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: update open delivery quantity even if a delivery block is set
entry.content:
Hi,

The business is asking to update open credit values even if a delivery block was set on schedule line of a sales order.

OSS note "741980 - Open credit values with delivery block in the sales order " does not meet business requierments. 

How can I change this standard behavior?

entry.title: Fix delivery dates for production in Sales Scheduling Agreements within frozen zone
entry.content:
My customer is using Sales Scheduling agreements, but is struggling with the integration to Production Planning and Procurement. When we create the SSA and enter the delivery schedule it will generate requirement that are supposed to be fixed when they are within a number of days within the delivery date. This "frozen zone" is about 30 days. How can we make sure that schedule lines with a date within the frozen zone cannot be changed? We are using the production go-ahead period, but that does not help us anywhere because it seems only for informational purposes. 

We are using SSA with delivery schedules and SSA with delivery orders (MAIS).

entry.title: Error message SIPT805 - " Total value is higher than in original invoice"
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

I am experiencing a problem when creating a credit note linked to an invoice in SAP SD.

When I try to save/post the document, the system displays an alert stating that the credit note amount exceeds the original invoice amount, and when I save it, no accounting document is generated and the credit note does not appear in VBRK or SIPT_VBRK.

However, upon checking in detail, the amounts appear to be exactly the same as those on the invoice.

Has anyone else experienced a similar issue?

Any suggestions on what else I should check would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: From where the payment method is getting fetched in sales order line item?
entry.content: I need to understand from where the payment method is getting fetched in sales order line item, as customer master data is having 'blank' payment method but still somehow in sales order line-item payment method 'direct debit' is getting picked automatically. I need to know which configuration is bringing this. because of this the invoice is getting created with 'direct debit' payment method & payment is also happening with 'direct debit' method, which doesn't need. Hence, I need to rectify the config and correct asap.

entry.title: Down Payment adjustment for Partial delivery/Partial invoice
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The customer is on SAP S/4 HANA On Premise solution- SAP S/4 HANA 2023-FPS 1

We have implemented Scope item BKJ- Sales order processing with Customer Down Payment. Now the requirement is to have the down payment adjusted for Partial invoices created against sales order line item. 

Scenario as below.

Order quantity - 100 EA. Delivery to be split on multiple delivery dates on the sales order.

Price per unit - 50 USD/ EA

Total order value - 5000 USD

Advance received - 500 USD (10% of order value)

Partial delivery/ invoice of 10 EA should adjust this 10% advance at the invoice and show an outstanding of only 450 USD against the total invoice value of 500 USD

Is there any Standard SAP solution for the On premise version 2023? 

I know that we can make use of the combination of Service material and condition type to provide a custom solution for this requirement but as far as possible we would want to avoid that.

Any help or pointers would be apprciated.

Thanks in advance.

Kartik Shah

entry.title: Sales Price Master Upload
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I attempted to upload sales pricing data using the Migration Cockpit. Specifically, this upload is for the unit price using a customized condition type (ZPR1). However, the system is throwing an error stating that the condition type ZPR1 is not supported by this migration object.

Could anyone please guide me on how to resolve this issue?

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: FOREIGN TRADE DATA: Function code cannot be selected
entry.content:
Hello,

Good day!

We encountered an error in the Foreign Trade Data item. When navigating through each description in the Incompleteness Analysis, we received the error “Function code cannot be selected” and the fields were disabled. 

The invoice in error is a cancellation invoice.

Could you please assist with the possible solution?

Please check the attached screenshot for reference.

Thank you!

entry.title: While executing WB2R_SV for a Sales commission contract it is giving pricing error in SAP S4 hana
entry.content:
Hi All

In the Sandbox SAP S4 hana 2023 on premise system while executing the WB2r_SV for external Sales commission contract I am getting the below error.

Sales commission contract:

Settlement procedure

and the below is SD pricing procedure for SO and Billing

Let me know if you need any further details.

Other Information: Created the sales order, delivery and invoice and External partner also is part of the sales order and invoice.

Successfully tested the Sales Rebate testing without any error with above SD pricing procedure and relevant Rebate Settlement pricing procedure. I don't think any issues with SD pricing procedure but not sure about the Sales commission settlement procedure.

Thanks

Sri

entry.title: Batch invoicing job for delivery plus sales order with same WBS or same linked Sales order
entry.content:
Hi Members,

I have requirement to implement an automated invoicing routine that automatically identifies and uses the pair of Sales Order and Outbound to create the billing document. The routine will also update the Billing Document's output type EDI = Immediately and save it once again. 

This routine cannot take the customer PO as reference as in the past it created issues as it would combine all outbounds that were linked to the same customer PO and create a single invoice (lump sum).
This would cause rejection from our customer, as a single billing document per SO / Outbound is expected per delivery.
we need to introduce a batch invoicing job for delivery plus sales order with the same WBS element or same linked sales order for sales Org. 
 
I checked for "schedule billing creation" app but parameter on the app is not satisfied. 
 
Thanks and Regards,
Rajesh
 
.

entry.title: Correction of header data without changing item details for Poland credit memo
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a standard credit‑memo document type in Poland that permits corrections to header‑level data without altering the line‑item details, specifically for the purpose of addressing formal errors?

This clarification is required for our Poland implementation ticket, as we need to ensure compliance with local legal and accounting requirements while avoiding unnecessary changes to item‑level data.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,
Yoshinee Mootheeveeren

entry.title: Stock Allocation from Multiple MRP Area
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We have two MRP areas within same plant. One for sales processing with Plant 1000 as MRP area and Second MRP area (Plant 1000 & Location : XYDM) for Production orders where business can run MRP for independent requirements and continuously manufacture Make To Stock Products. Business will transfer the stock from XYDM location to other storage locations within 1000 plant regularly. 

During Sales order creation, availability will be performed within MRP area 1000 & stock available other than XYDM storage location will be reserved.  Business wants the automatic stock allocation from other MRP area also if stock not available in the main MRP area. The Products are batch managed. Recommended the customer to add the storage location in the Sales order so that the stock from XYDM location can be considered. Business team not accepting manual intervention and expecting auto allocation.

Customer using S4 HANA Public Cloud system and Basic ATP enabled. Customer not ready to spend for aATP.
